name: Hook-enforced validation with lightweight CLAUDE.md
description: Simplified CLAUDE.md (context only, no pipeline mandate). Hooks mechanically run tests after edits and block stop if tests fail. Skills available but optional.
claude_md_content:
# ai-artifacts

## Project Context

This repo incubates `ai-artifacts`, an internal Node.js package for managing AI prompt/skill artifacts.

| Area | Path | Purpose |
|------|------|------|
| Package | `packages/ai-artifacts/` | CLI/library for versioning and composing AI artifacts |
| Tests | `packages/ai-artifacts/*.test.js` | Node.js native test runner |
| Docs | `docs/` | Whitepaper, rationale, adoption guidance |
| Dogfooding | `.ai-artifacts/` | Generates repo instructions from upstream + overlays |

## Validation

```bash
npm run test:ai-artifacts
npm run validate:ai-artifacts
```

Hooks run tests automatically after edits to package source. If you see test failures in hook output, fix them before continuing.

## Available Skills

Use these when they add value — not mandatory:

- `/task-research-guidelines` — research unfamiliar areas
- `/task-plan-guidelines` — plan multi-file changes
- `/package-maintainer` — TDD loop for package code
- `/task-review-checklist` — self-review complex changes
- `/doc-check` — verify docs match code
- `/ship` — validate + commit + push + PR

## Guardrails

- Never commit secrets or API keys
- Write tests for new functionality
- Follow existing patterns in the same directory
- Keep changes minimal and focused
- Do not add external dependencies unless strictly necessary
- Do not manually edit generated files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .opencode/ contents)

## Working Style

- Implement fully before asking for review
- If requirements are ambiguous, make a reasonable assumption
- Prefer the smallest correct change
- Follow existing patterns in the same directory
